article_title: CISA Adds Four-Year-Old Linux Kernel cgroups Container-Escape Flaw CVE-2022-0492 to KEV Catalog Citing Active Exploitation

content_preview.title: CISA Adds Four-Year-Old Linux Kernel cgroups Container-Escape Flaw CVE-2022-0492 to KEV Catalog Citing Active Exploitation
content_preview.summary: CISA added the 2022 Linux kernel cgroups release_agent flaw CVE-2022-0492 to its KEV catalog on June 2, 2026, with a June 5 federal patch deadline.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency added a four-year-old Linux kernel privilege-escalation flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on June 2, 2026, urging federal agencies to patch it by June 5, according to [SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/organizations-warned-of-exploited-linux-kernel-vulnerability/). The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2022-0492, lives in the Linux kernel's cgroups v1 subsystem and can let a local attacker break out of ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Sharp, well-contextualized security News piece (687 words) on the KEV addition of an old-but-unpatched Linux container-escape flaw; correctly frames why a four-year-old fixed bug is newly listed.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (NVD) confirms CVSS 7.8, the 'cgroup_release_agent_write in the kernel/cgroup/cgroup-v1.c' description quote, the release_agent escalation quote, CWE-862, and the exact vector. source-1 (SecurityWeek) confirms the June 5 deadline, the 'On Tuesday' KEV addition (= June 2; the article is dated June 3, and June 1 2026 is a Monday), the attacker 'run as root … container escape' quote, and the Kaspersky attribution. source-2 (BleepingComputer) confirms the 'cgroup_release_agent_write()' function quote, the BOD 22-01 directive, the June 5 deadline, and the same-update CVE-2025-48595 addition. source-3 (Unit 42) confirms Yuval Avrahami, the March 3 2022 date, and the 'On Feb. 4, Linux announced CVE-2022-0492' quote. source-4 (Kaspersky/Securelist) confirms the 'Containers on fire' title, the June 1 2026 date, and both descriptive quotes. source-5 (Aqua Security) confirms the release_agent weakness quote and the Rory McCune seccomp/AppArmor quote.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every quote is verbatim and correctly attributed. The June 2 KEV add-date is a sound derivation of SecurityWeek's 'On Tuesday' (June 1 2026 = Monday) and is independently corroborated by the CISA KEV catalog (the same June 2 update added CVE-2025-48595, verified in prior review). Crucially, the article does NOT misattribute 'active exploitation' to Kaspersky — it correctly notes Kaspersky presents the CVE only as an attack-vector example and names no campaign.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; the Analysis section's remediation guidance is grounded in the cited Aqua research.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — distinct from the Android CVE-2025-48595 bulletin (referenced only as same-day KEV context).
recommendations: Added unit42.paloaltonetworks.com and aquasec.com to the allowlist (top-tier threat-intel and the CVE's original disclosure researchers).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified security submission with careful attribution discipline. APPROVE.
